title: lyonia truncata subsp. montecristina
intro: Lyonia truncata subsp. montecristina, also known as mountain lyonia, is a plant species belonging to the family Ericaceae. It is a shrub with fragrant leaves and clusters of white flowers, native to the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The leaves and stems of mountain lyonia can be used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to offer health benefits including antioxidant and immune-boosting properties.
